failure_modes.text: Plans with conjunctive structure can produce unwarranted optimism because every step must go right.
failure_modes.mitigation: Break conjunctive plans into independent probability assessments for each step and multiply them; the compound probability is almost always lower than intuition suggests.

failure_modes.text: Growth can fail to compound when hidden fragility in quality or incentives erodes the engine.
failure_modes.source_quote: ignore fragility in incentives, quality, retention, or operational resilience
failure_modes.mitigation: Monitor the compounding engine for hidden quality or incentive decay that erodes the base before the next round of growth.

failure_modes.text: Boiling the ocean can waste effort instead of stacking the highest-leverage gains.
failure_modes.mitigation: Apply the Pareto principle: stack the highest-leverage gains first rather than boiling the ocean with equal effort across all fronts.

premortem_questions.text: What base are we protecting so gains can keep stacking instead of resetting?
premortem_questions.source_quote: protecting downside is as important as amplifying upside

premortem_questions.text: What single failure could erase years of accumulated progress?
How does this work advance the problem instead of merely creating activity?
premortem_questions.source_quote: How does what you're doing solve the problem?

heuristics.text: Stack repeated improvements where gains persist into the next cycle.
heuristics.source_quote: results generated in one cycle become the base for generating results in the next cycle

heuristics.text: Use structured iterative loops so learning compounds rather than restarting from scratch.
heuristics.source_quote: setting goals, identifying problems, diagnosing root causes, designing solutions, and implementing them

heuristics.text: Focus time on highly leveraged activities that compound output over time.
